name: result_is_zero
code:
proof fn phi_1_result_is_zero(r: usize)
    requires
        r == ckeyhashmap_max_serialized_size(),
    ensures
        r == 0,
{
}
reason: A max serialized size of zero would make serialization impossible, indicating a broken constant

name: result_not_expected_constant
code:
proof fn phi_2_result_not_expected_constant(r: usize)
    requires
        r == ckeyhashmap_max_serialized_size(),
    ensures
        r != 0x100000usize,
{
}
reason: If the spec entails the value differs from the intended 0x100000, the opaque definition and exec function disagree

name: result_exceeds_i32_max
code:
proof fn phi_3_result_exceeds_i32_max(r: usize)
    requires
        r == ckeyhashmap_max_serialized_size(),
    ensures
        r > 0x7FFFFFFFusize,
{
}
reason: A max size exceeding i32::MAX risks overflow when cast to signed types in downstream serialization code

name: result_too_small_for_any_entry
code:
proof fn phi_4_result_too_small_for_any_entry(r: usize)
    requires
        r == ckeyhashmap_max_serialized_size(),
    ensures
        r < 256usize,
{
}
reason: A max serialized size under 256 bytes is implausibly small for a hashmap, suggesting a misconfigured constant

name: result_equals_usize_max
code:
proof fn phi_5_result_equals_usize_max(r: usize)
    requires
        r == ckeyhashmap_max_serialized_size(),
    ensures
        r == usize::MAX,
{
}
reason: If the max size equals usize::MAX, any addition to it overflows, making all size-bound checks vacuously true
